response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished generalist software engineer with 15+ years across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/low-latency systems, which satisfies the experience-years path for the educational requirement. However, this is a Principal Performance Architect role centered on SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. The relevant adjacencies (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang microservices, a C++ wave-field-synthesis thesis) are tangential and dated, not architecture-level hardware work. The core required and preferred qualifications are essentially unmet.
strengths: Deep, sustained software engineering experience and proven ability to learn new domains and languages quickly
Some low-level/performance exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, high-throughput async DLang services, C++ audio thesis
Strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) plus demonstrated communication via talks, workshops, and open source
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or system-architecture-for-silicon experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Profile is web/full-stack-centric; performance work is application-level, not hardware/accelerator performance analysis
